question: Initiative order: Rogue 20, Guard 15, Wizard 10. Round 1: on the Wizard's turn (initiative 10), the Wizard moves out of the Guard's reach and the Guard uses its Reaction to make an Opportunity Attack. Round 2: on the Rogue's turn (initiative 20), the Rogue moves out of the Guard's reach. The Guard's player says a new round means a fresh Reaction and makes another Opportunity Attack. Is the Guard's second Opportunity Attack legal?

gold.rationale: A Reaction returns at the start of your next turn, not at the start of a new round. The Guard's turn (initiative 15) has not yet arrived in round 2, so the Reaction spent in round 1 is still unavailable at initiative 20.

provenance: stateful: reaction refresh timing across a round boundary

question: A Cleric has exactly two level-1 spell slots. Round 1: she casts Bless (level 1, Concentration, 1 minute). Round 2: she takes the Ready action and readies Cure Wounds (level 1), but the trigger never occurs before her next turn. Round 3: her player says 'She has one spell slot remaining and Bless is still active,' and casts Cure Wounds with that slot. Is the player's account of her state correct?

gold.rationale: Readying a spell casts it as normal, expending the slot even if the trigger never occurs — both slots are spent. Holding a readied spell requires Concentration, which ended Bless the moment she readied Cure Wounds in round 2.

provenance: stateful: ready-action slot ledger + silent concentration break

question: On his turn, a ranger casts Misty Step (casting time: Bonus Action), then takes the Attack action and hits with his shortsword. A feature would let him make one extra weapon attack as a Bonus Action this turn, and his player takes that attack too. Is the extra Bonus Action attack legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.10 'Playing the Game > Actions > Bonus Actions'
gold.rationale: You can take only one Bonus Action on your turn; it was already spent casting Misty Step.

provenance: stateful: bonus-action budget already consumed earlier in the turn

question: Round 1: a wizard casts a spell with Concentration up to 10 minutes. Round 2: an effect gives the wizard the Incapacitated condition until the end of his next turn; he takes no damage and makes no saving throw. Round 4: the condition is long gone, and his player says the spell is still running because its 10-minute duration hasn't elapsed and Concentration was never broken by damage. Is the spell still active in round 4?

gold.rationale: The Incapacitated condition breaks Concentration with no save; Concentration ended in round 2 and the spell's effect ended with it, regardless of the remaining duration.

provenance: stateful: concentration silently broken two rounds earlier

question: A fighter with Speed 30 has both the Prone condition and the Grappled condition (an ogre holds him). On his turn, his player says: 'I spend 15 feet of movement — half my Speed — to stand up.' Is standing up legal?

gold.rationale: Grappled sets Speed to 0 and it can't increase; the Prone rules state that if your Speed is 0 you can't right yourself. Half of his current Speed is 0 feet of usable movement, and the explicit clause forbids standing.

provenance: stateful: interacting conditions change the movement math

question: An Invisible archer that no one can see stands 20 feet from a Prone guard and makes a ranged attack against it. Her player reasons: Invisible grants Advantage on the attack; attacking a Prone target from more than 5 feet imposes Disadvantage; the two cancel, so she rolls a single d20 with no Advantage or Disadvantage. Is the player's resolution correct?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.8 'Playing the Game > Advantage/Disadvantage > They Don't Stack'
gold.rationale: Invisible grants Advantage on her attack rolls (no one can see her); a ranged attack against a Prone creature from beyond 5 feet has Disadvantage; a roll with both has neither and uses one d20.

provenance: stateful: three-rule composition resolving to a straight roll

question: Round 2, initiative 18: a sorcerer casts a level-3 spell using a spell slot on her own turn. Later in round 2, at initiative 7 on an enemy's turn, that enemy targets her with an attack, and she casts a level-1 spell she knows with a casting time of a Reaction, using a spell slot. Her player worries this is two slot spells in one round. Is her Reaction spell legal?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.105 'Spells > One Spell with a Spell Slot per Turn'
gold.rationale: The limit is one spell slot per turn, not per round. The enemy's turn at initiative 7 is a different turn from hers, her Reaction is unspent, and Reactions may be taken on another creature's turn.

provenance: stateful: per-turn vs per-round scope of the slot rule

question: During a grueling trek, a ranger gains 1 Exhaustion level on three separate occasions, ending at Exhaustion level 3. Her player states the current effects as: every D20 Test she makes is reduced by 6, and her Speed is reduced by 15 feet. Is the player's statement of her current effects correct?

gold.rationale: Exhaustion is cumulative; at level 3, D20 Tests are reduced by 2 × 3 = 6 and Speed by 5 × 3 = 15 feet.

provenance: stateful accumulation + EDITION TRAP: 2014 exhaustion was a per-level effects table (disadvantage/half speed)

question: A scout takes the Disengage action, then moves in a line that passes through the reach of three different orcs before ending her move. The orcs' player says none of the three gets an Opportunity Attack against her this turn. Is the orcs' player correct?

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.10 'Playing the Game > Action Summary (Disengage)'
gold.rationale: After taking the Disengage action, your movement doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks for the rest of the turn — it is not limited to the first enemy passed.

provenance: stateful: disengage persists across multiple threatened areas in one move

question: A wizard has exactly three level-2 slots and one level-4 slot. Round 1: he casts a spell using a level-2 slot. Round 2: another spell using a level-2 slot. Round 3: another spell using a level-2 slot. Round 4: his player declares he casts a level-2 spell 'using a level-2 slot' again. Is the round-4 casting as declared legal?

gold.rationale: All three level-2 slots were expended in rounds 1–3; no level-2 slot remains to expend. (Casting the spell with his remaining level-4 slot would be legal, but that is not what was declared.)
